summary: Cycle K (1st cron fire of UTC 2026-05-25; cycle J closed 2026-05-24T20:02:03Z, cycle K opens 2026-05-25T01:01:00Z = ~4h 59min wall-clock gap — THIRD consecutive non-~1h gap, strongly weakening any prior ~1h dominant-mode hypothesis). Ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 45th consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED at 01:01:31Z, round-trip 0 failures sustained (28s elapsed — NEW +4s tick above 5-cycle 24s plateau D+F+G+H+J; NOT explainable by concurrent Founder commit; alternate confound: heavy aggregator workload — 122-line session-transcript-summary churn + 141-line post-commit-hook.log auto-clean; heartbeat written 01:01:52Z status=PASS 4-field schema). THREE GENUINE NEW OBSERVATIONS: (1) CADENCE FURTHER WEAKENED — J→K gap = ~5h, THIRD consecutive non-~1h gap. Thirteen observations total: M→A ~5h, A→B ~53min, B→C ~60.5min, C→D ~59min, D→E ~59min, E→F ~60min, F→G ~60min, G→H ~60min, H→I ~3h, I→J ~5h, J→K ~5h. Diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADED from cycle J's 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h' to 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 10 post-A gaps; the ~1h cluster has given way to non-~1h gaps in the most recent window — possible regime change or pre-existing multimodality not previously visible'. Five reframes considered including NEW regime-change hypothesis. (2) HEARTBEAT DURATION CONFOUND MODEL REFINED TO DUAL-CLASS — cycle I (27s) was attributed to concurrent-Founder-commit confound; cycle K (28s) has NO concurrent commit (last non-cron 4.5-10h prior) but heavy aggregator workload (122+141 line churn). Two distinct confound classes now: (a) concurrent-commit at cycle I, (b) heavy-aggregator at cycle K. Don't over-fit single-cause when a second outlier in a different category emerges. (3) FOUNDER CLEARED DIRTY TREE in J→K window — commit ff14d2d1 at 2026-05-24T20:34:34Z (~32min after cycle J close) cleared the 7 carry-over untracked items preserved across cycles G→J; additionally scripts/sentry-fetch-events.mjs (untracked at cycle J open) is now tracked. Working tree at cycle K open: ZERO untracked items (first clean state in 7+ cycles). Carry-over #6 CLOSED. TOKEN THRESHOLD PERSISTS 9th cross-cycle cycle — cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~380k (was 345k at cycle J; +35k cycle K). thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] PRESERVED. Founder-decision still LIVE for 10th consecutive cycle. WELLNESS: status=active throughout. Counters: tokens accumulate continuously (~380k cumulative, threshold-crossed-persisted 9th cross-cycle cycle); hours_active discrete-context (0.5h). Single yellow user-context-gate continues (14333.9 min, ~9.95 days). Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST. Three genuine new observations corroborated by independent evidence (git log + heartbeat-file delta + wall-clock + working-tree diff + git ls-files). The cycle K refinement (dual-class confound model) is metric integrity working as designed — refine the model when a second outlier in a different category emerges, don't dismiss the data or over-fit the original single-cause hypothesis. Eight carry-over Founder action items preserved from cycle J close; #1 (cadence) further downgraded with three-consecutive-outlier note; #6 (untracked tree preservation) CLOSED by Founder commit ff14d2d1; #7 (heartbeat duration) refined to dual-confound-class model; #8 (token-counter) persistence-flag preserved 9th cross-cycle cycle.
founder_action_items: Cron cadence — 29th observation; diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADED from cycle J's 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; insufficient evidence to claim any specific schedule' to 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 10 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; the ~1h cluster has given way to non-~1h gaps in the most recent window — possible regime change or pre-existing multimodality not previously visible'. J→K gap = ~5h is the THIRD consecutive non-~1h gap (after H→I ~3h, I→J ~5h). Thirteen observations: M→A ~5h, A→B ~53min, B→C ~60.5min, C→D ~59min, D→E ~59min, E→F ~60min, F→G ~60min, G→H ~60min, H→I ~3h, I→J ~5h, J→K ~5h. Five reframes considered: (a) event-triggered (no Founder activity in J→K argues against), (b) Founder-active windows suppress cron (does not explain J→K), (c) wider multimodal cluster, (d) original ~1h schedule with missed fires (would need 13 missed fires across three consecutive gaps, increasingly implausible), (e) NEW regime change (early ~1h dominant, recent non-~1h dominant). Founder-action: inspect cron schedule source OR add Step-0 guard.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) — carry-over from cycles S→J; rolling-window mechanics.

main-flows.html user-context capture ~9.95 days stale at cycle K (was 9.74 at cycle J; +299.7 min consistent with ~5h J→K gap) — run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close.
quota-status weekly_cap field still null — no % computation possible (preserved from cycles R→J).

post-commit aggregate-self-tests FAIL on 2 aggregators per cycle F's metric-integrity correction: aggregate-test-health parity-fail (source-detected but status=unknown) + aggregate-security-health stale-timestamp. Founder-action: investigate whether test-health source-detection / status-mapping is broken, and why security-health timestamp is stale. Carry-over wording locked at cycle F.
A12_operational — sustained yellow with continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics — CONFOUND MODEL REFINED TO DUAL-CLASS at cycle K. Cycle I (27s) attributed to concurrent-Founder-commit confound; cycle K (28s) has NO concurrent commit but heavy aggregator workload (122-line session-transcript-summary churn + 141-line post-commit-hook.log auto-clean). Two distinct confound classes now: (a) concurrent-commit, (b) heavy-aggregator. Founder-action remains: inspect .husky/post-commit source AND post-commit-hook.log entries for non-routine commits to determine whether the hook wrote GATE-FAIL or PASS, and reconcile with the 4-field PASS schema written by cron's regen-all path. Additional research: characterize heavy-aggregator workload boundary (when does churn cross the threshold that adds +4s).

Wellness token-counter semantics — THRESHOLD PERSISTS 9th cross-cycle cycle. Cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~380k (was 345k at cycle J; +35k cycle K). thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] PRESERVED. Projection method continues to track across FIVE checkpoints now (E:102.5% of 200k, H:95% of 300k, I:105% of 300k, J:~115% of 300k, K:~127% of 300k). Founder-decision still LIVE for 10th cycle: (a) reset tokens per cron fire, (b) raise threshold, (c) auto-trigger rest when crossed-while-active, (d) leave current convention (current path).
dropped_action_items_this_cycle: Untracked tree preservation (7 items + scripts/sentry-fetch-events.mjs) — CLOSED by Founder commit ff14d2d1 'chore: clean dirty tree — iter46 captures + overnight reports + stop-decisions' at 2026-05-24T20:34:34Z (~32min after cycle J close). Working tree at cycle K open: zero untracked. Carry-over #6 from cycles G→J resolved.
metric_integrity_corrections_this_cycle: Heartbeat-duration confound model REFINEMENT — cycle J validated cycle I's concurrent-commit hypothesis via natural experiment (cycle I had concurrent commit → 27s; cycle J had no concurrent commit → 24s baseline). Cycle K extends the experiment: NO concurrent commit but +4s tick to 28s. Original single-class hypothesis (concurrent commit explains all ticks) is too narrow. Refined model: dual-class confound (concurrent-commit class A at cycle I; heavy-aggregator class B at cycle K). Don't over-fit single-cause when a second outlier in a different category emerges. The 5-cycle 24s plateau (D+F+G+H+J) still holds; cycle I and cycle K are two distinct documented exceptions.

Cadence diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADE — cycle J downgraded from cycle I's 'bimodal/multimodal with possible mid-mode at ~3h' to 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; insufficient evidence to claim any specific schedule'. Cycle K's J→K ~5h gap (THIRD consecutive non-~1h gap) further weakens any cluster claim and surfaces a NEW reframe (regime change): early window (A→B through G→H) was ~1h dominant; recent window (H→I onward) is non-~1h dominant. Honest cycle K diagnosis: 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 10 post-A gaps; ~1h cluster has given way to non-~1h gaps in the most recent window — possible regime change or pre-existing multimodality not previously visible'. Five reframes carried, one new.
